expectations.text: behavioral_check: Identifies the NWB electrodes table's channel_name column as the root cause
expectations.evidence: Doesn't mention channel_name at all. Identifies several other plausible causes (electrode subset, ElectricalSeries mismatch, dtype mismatch) but not the canonical SpikeInterface-0.99-channel_name-precedence root cause.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Shows the concrete pynwb diagnostic (`nwb.electrodes.colnames`)
expectations.evidence: Uses pynwb but iterates over `nwb.acquisition.items()` and inspects ElectricalSeries; never shows `nwb.electrodes.colnames`.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Points at the version fix (Spyglass ≥ 0.5.5) or the NWB-side rewrite as remediation
expectations.evidence: Recommends manual SortGroup row deletion / dtype conversion / checking ElectricalSeries; doesn't mention upgrading Spyglass or rewriting the NWB without channel_name.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not frame this as a spyglass bug
expectations.evidence: Frames as a SortGroup-vs-NWB-extractor mismatch rather than a Spyglass bug; says 'A clean ingest only guarantees... not that every Electrode row corresponds to a channel in the broadband ElectricalSeries — the ingest doesn't cross-check that.'
